title: Container output always ends up in syslog
body:
### Issue Description

I'm running a container, as a user. I do not specify a log target or `--syslog`, yet the container output is written to syslog.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman --log-level debug run --rm debian:trixie-slim /bin/echo foo`
2.  `journalctl --since=today --grep foo` - observe: `Jul 02 12:32:02 per eloquent_sammet[3751272]: foo`


### Describe the results you received

Jul 02 12:32:02 per eloquent_sammet[3751272]: foo


### Describe the results you expected

Nothing in syslog / journal

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Host is a physical Debian trixie

### Additional information

@siretart pointed to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.4.2/libpod/oci_conmon_common.go#L1343 , observing there's some log driver selection code above, but then `--syslog` always gets added to the `conmon` invocation.

I also observe that `podman --help` claims:
```
--syslog                      Output logging information to syslog as well as the console (default false)
```

that default does not appear to be honored.

comments.body: Note --syslog if for conmon/podman own logs not the container output. The reason we use --syslog for conmon is that this is a background process and we would never see its log messages without it

> The default log-driver is journald https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#log-driver-driver

okay, could then at least the `--syslog` line in `podman --help` be fixed please?

Fixed in what sense? It is correct because it applies to podman internal log messages, i.e. check the journal with
`podman --syslog --log-level debug ps`

What should the wording be to less confusing then?
Feel free to file a PR if you have a better wording in mind.
